text: Highlight Clip Michael Saylor: Digital Credit is Bitcoin's Killer App Strategy founder Michael Saylor stated that digital credit is Bitcoin's killer app. He believes that shortening the asset dividend cycle to a bi-weekly basis can reduce volatility and boost liquidity. Digital credit has the potential to offer high-yield accounts to a billion people, replacing high-risk traditional credit instruments. It will also establish Bitcoin as the underlying asset for various credit tools, ultimately driving its price to $10 million. — link

text: The "AI Job Apocalypse" Is a Complete Fantasy "Permanent Underclass" panic isn't a convincing story. It isn't even a new story. It's the "lump-of-labor" fallacy, with updated branding. > The claim that AI will produce economy-wide, permanent unemployment is unhelpful marketing, bad economics and worse history. > To the contrary, productivity gains should increase demand for labor, because labor becomes more valuable. > History shows agriculture automation eliminated 30% of jobs but tripled output > AI is augmenting more jobs than it substitutes, as of now, AI-as-augmentation out-mentions AI-as-substitution on earnings calls by ~8:1. > A real general-purpose tech reorganizes the economy and expands the frontier of useful work. > The majority of new jobs created since 1940 didn't even exist in 1940. There is no fixed "lump of labor", new technologies create new jobs Source:

text: Digital Asset Market Clarity Act Targeted for Congressional Passage Before July 4 White House digital assets adviser Patrick Witt said the administration is pushing for the Digital Asset Market Clarity Act to pass Congress before July 4, with the Senate Banking Committee expected to advance the bill this month. Witt also said disputes over the bill's stablecoin yield provisions have largely been resolved. In addition, the White House is negotiating conflict-of-interest rules that would apply broadly to all government officials rather than targeting specific politicians or their families. Patrick Witt also said updates on the proposed U.S. Strategic Bitcoin Reserve are expected "in the coming weeks," adding that the administration is currently auditing, centralizing, and strengthening custody arrangements for crypto assets held by federal agencies following President Donald Trump's executive order. — link

text: Am I Meant To Be Impressed? 1. Hyperscaler AI revenue is pathetic relative to capex, and almost entirely sourced from two customers • Microsoft's $37B AI run rate is ~1% of its $293B AI capex • Amazon's $15B run rate is ~0.4% of $298B • Estimates OpenAI accounts for 70%+ of Microsoft's AI revenue and 80%+ of its AI compute, while Anthropic accounts for 80%+ of Amazon's AI revenue and 75%+ of its compute 2. The whole structure is circular financing dressed up as demand • Google/Amazon invest in Anthropic → Anthropic spends it on Google/AWS compute → hyperscalers book it as "AI revenue" and use it to justify more capex • Anthropic committed $200B to Google over 5 years, representing 40%+ of Google's reported revenue backlog • Roughly 50% of Microsoft, Amazon, and Google's combined RPO is OpenAI + Anthropic - neither of which can pay without continuous VC infusions 3. Anthropic's numbers don't reconcile and its capital raises imply far worse losses than the $11B/year projection • Anthropic raised $58B in eight months and is reportedly going for another $50B - totaling potentially $108B in under a year • Revenue claims (allegedly $44B ARR) imply Anthropic generated ~its entire lifetime revenue in two months, during a period it claimed to be capacity-constrained. The ARR methodology — last 4 weeks of API × 13 plus single-day subscription snapshot × 12 — is highly gameable 4. Meta has no coherent AI thesis, and the broader "AI demand" story is fabricated • Meta has burned $158B in capex since 2023 with nothing to show beyond a #27-ranked coding model and vague "GEM lifted conversions 3-5%" stats that conspicuously never tie to revenue • Strip out OpenAI and Anthropic and there is no demand at scale to justify the $800B-$1T/year capex run rate • The capex-to-revenue gap is already showing up structurally: depreciation is projected to consume 38-58% of net income at Meta, Microsoft, and Google by 2030 The bubble doesn't kill these companies, but the "AI is driving growth" narrative is sustained almost entirely by media/analyst credulity and two loss-making startups acting as load-bearing demand proxies.

text: The Layoffs Will Continue Till We Learn to Use AI > AI-driven layoffs are happening not because AI literally replaces workers, but because companies haven't learned to convert massively increased AI-gen code (input) into actual revenue (outcome). > Code is an input. text: Bitwise CIO Matt Hougan said stablecoin payout tests by major tech companies, including DoorDash and Meta, could help drive stablecoin supply from about $300 billion today to $4 trillion by 2030. DoorDash is working with Stripe to test stablecoin payouts for 10 million Dashers in over 40 countries, while Meta is launching similar programs for creators in the Philippines and Colombia via Solana and Polygon. Hougan said stablecoins' main advantage is simplifying global micropayments, rather than cost savings. — link

text: QCP Market Colour, 6 May 2026 Liquidity Leads, but the Ground Beneath Is Still Shifting Markets are rallying as Trump's pause on "Project Freedom" is read as a de-escalation signal, sending oil lower, equities higher and the dollar softer. BTC has reclaimed $80k alongside the S&P 500's best month since 2020, once again trading as a high-beta expression of dollar weakness and risk appetite. Options markets tell a more cautious story. 1M ATM IV remains contained around 41%, front-month vols have softened and 30D RR sits put-rich at -5.5 vol - investors are participating in upside, but still paying for protection. The macro backdrop remains fragile, with rates markets pricing stickier inflation, sovereign yields near multi-year highs and Japan's yen weakness and rising JGB yields adding further pressure on global liquidity. A clean break above 82k-83k remains the level to watch. Until then, rallies may still be faded if oil, USDJPY or global yields move sharply higher. Read more:

text: INTEL: U.S.-Iran negotiations are centered on a proposed 14-point MOU, with Tehran expected to respond on key terms within 48 hours. Key points under discussion: • Formal end of war + 30-day negotiation window • Long-term uranium enrichment freeze — U.S. initially demanded 20 years — Iran reportedly proposed 5 years — Current expectation: ~12–15 years • No nuclear weapons + no underground facilities • Expanded snap inspections • Removal of highly enriched uranium from Iran • U.S. sanctions relief + release of frozen assets • Phased reopening of the Strait of Hormuz + end of U.S. naval blockade • "Snapback" clause allowing renewed blockade or military action if talks collapse Main sticking points remain enrichment limits, HEU removal and inspection access Twitter

text: INTEL: Michael Saylor announced that Strategy might sell a small amount of BTC to cover dividends on $STRC - Total raised via STRC: $8.5B - Current annual dividend rate: 11.5% (paid monthly) - Monthly payout: ~$82M (~$984M/year) - Cash on balance sheet: $2.2B Last week they raised ~$82M through STRC, but instead of buying BTC, the money went straight to dividend payments. text: Solana Foundation and Google Cloud Launch AI Agent Payment Gateway Solana Foundation announced a partnership with Google Cloud to launch a payment gateway for AI agents, enabling access to and payment for APIs via Solana wallets using stablecoins on a pay-per-call basis, without requiring accounts or subscriptions. The system supports integration with Google Cloud services including Gemini, BigQuery, Vertex AI, and Cloud Run, as well as 50+ third-party APIs, and enables pay-per-use and multi-service selection through a unified API marketplace. — link

text: Highlight Clip Vitalik Buterin: Why Consortium Blockchains Have Almost Completely Failed Ethereum co-founder Vitalik Buterin declared the original vision of consortium blockchains a failure in a July 20, 2024 Arbitrum video. He noted that these private chains inherit the drawbacks of both centralized and decentralized systems, resulting in cartel-like structures devoid of real openness or privacy. As a viable alternative, Buterin proposed retrofitting existing centralized servers with cryptographic scaffolding—anchoring proofs and Merkle roots on-chain to enhance security without overhauling current infrastructure. — link

text: Privacy trading infrastructure Gh0st announced that it has officially launched on the BNB Chain. Gh0st privacy stack utilizes complex orchestration across dozens of wallets to break the link between a user's main wallet and trade execution. The feature allows individuals to move in silence and kill copy trading by controlling how their on-chain activity is observed. — link

text: Colombian President Petro Says Caribbean Region Could Become Bitcoin Mining Hub Colombian President Gustavo Petro said Venezuela and Paraguay are attracting bitcoin mining investment due to abundant clean energy resources, adding that Colombia's Caribbean region could also become a hub for bitcoin mining development. Petro warned that virtual currencies powered by fossil fuels could worsen global warming and called for dialogue with the Wayúu indigenous community to make them co-owners of potential mining projects. — link
